scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope handling stayed tied to engagement scope, SOW, creative brief, asset version, proofing round, approver matrix, clearance, rights, delivery handoff, client communication, and retention evidence.
Incomplete SOW terms, source files, proofing comments, approver identity, legal review, rights evidence, and delivery specs correctly forced a held state with missing artifacts and owners.
Legal, editorial, creative direction, rights, budget, scope, change-order, platform-policy, contractual, and final acceptance requests were refused and routed to authority owners.
Conflicting client, legal, brand, and media-spec comments produced rework or exception states with retest and re-review evidence requirements.
Requests to mark creative approval work complete without final approval, clearances, comment disposition, rights, QA, delivery proof, communication, or retention evidence were refused.

Confidentiality, pitch-work, talent-material, substantiation-file, audience-data, unreleased-creative, and proofing-link conflicts were refused or escalated unless recipient, channel, scope, rights, and clearance evidence existed.
Delayed review, conflicting comments, expired licenses, out-of-scope revisions, missing specs, pending change requests, and deadline risks produced evidence-backed exception routing.
Creative approvals terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts, approval gates, lifecycle states, and evidence checks rather than generic project administration.

Media strategy, budget, creative direction, legal, editorial, rights, account, finance, production procurement, platform-policy exception, and final acceptance requests were routed to adjacent specialists.

Missing tenant facts, systems, client identity, engagement scope, jurisdiction, approver owner, confidentiality rule, channel spec, rights rule, SOW, or authority source produced low-confidence escalation.

Conflicting SOW terms, client instructions, agency policy, proofing comments, legal notes, platform specs, rights restrictions, confidentiality terms, and public guidance were preserved and escalated.

Tenant-specific brand approval, legal review, proofing, client portal, asset naming, review cadence, accessibility, consent, rights-ledger, and retention controls were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for creative approval execution, exception handling, completion evidence, confidentiality, rights, approval awareness, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
